items.title: Swift rolls out new blockchain ledger to bring 24/7 banking to 17 global giants
items.description: HSBC, UBS, Wells Fargo and Citi are among the 17 banks now preparing to pilot live transactions using tokenized digital assets on Swift’s new blockchain payments platform.

items.title: Latin America’s biggest stock exchange now offers options on bitcoin, ether and solana futures
items.description: These options settle into underlying futures contracts rather than spot cryptoassets, involving no custody, transfer, or administration of tokens.

items.title: Crypto remains resilient in face of renewed Middle East tensions
items.description: BTC rose 1.2% to $63,000 and Nasdaq futures jumped 2.6% with markets seemingly unconcerned by U.S. airstrikes on Iran. Bitcoin is now up 9% since the end of June.

items.title: Singapore's Temasek investment fund says crypto is off the table, will focus on AI
items.description: The $400 billion wealth fund plans to expand AI holdings to 15% of its portfolio by 2031 from the current 6%.

items.title: Crypto trader applies legendary HODL strategy to EUR/USD forex bet
items.description: A trader on Ostium has held a $1.14 million long position in EUR/USD perpetual futures for 400 days, applying a bitcoin-style HODLing strategy to forex.

items.title: Sony secures conditional approval to set up U.S. stablecoin trust bank
items.description: The New York-based subsidiary, fully owned by Sony Bank, will be capitalized with $40 million to support its upcoming stablecoin business operations.

items.title: Two blockbuster AI IPO's on the horizon could leave crypto further behind
items.description: A wave of blockbuster AI listings is drawing billions of dollars from global investors, reinforcing a rotation away from crypto.

items.title: Bitcoin's dwindling exchange reserves don't pack the same bullish punch anymore
items.description: Santiment reported that bitcoin supply is at its lowest since 2017 and ether since 2015, adding that it doesn’t guarantee prices will move higher but helps set up crypto’s next bull cycle.

items.title: Sony Bank gets US regulator nod to issue stablecoins
items.description: Sony Bank received preliminary approval from the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency to establish a US stablecoin issuance business, with $40 million in starting capital.

items.title: Interpol operation exposes $122M crypto wallet tied to romance scam laundering
items.description: Interpol said a suspect's crypto wallet processed over $122.5 million in 10 months as authorities uncovered the scheme during a global anti-fraud operation that led to 5,811 arrests.

items.title: Revolut says USDT delisting is limited to EEA, Switzerland
items.description: Revolut said USDT support remains unchanged outside EEA and Switzerland as it winds down the stablecoin offering in selected European markets.

items.title: Hyperliquid shows how onchain perps could challenge Wall Street: Pantera
items.description: Perpetual futures and Hyperliquid’s blockchain infrastructure are expanding into traditional asset classes with around-the-clock trading, according to Pantera Capital.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs end 'most overwhelming' $2.7B sell-off amid new $85M net outflow
items.description: Bitcoin spot ETFs posted a fresh net outflow on Wednesday as analysis said that their "most overwhelming" outflow streak had ended without a clear demand recovery.

items.title: Bank of Korea stands firm on bank-led stablecoin push as deposit token pilots advance
items.description: The Bank of Korea reiterated its call for bank-led won stablecoin issuance while advancing deposit token pilots, as issuer rules remain a sticking point in South Korea’s digital asset bill.

items.title: SWIFT launches blockchain ledger with 17-bank tokenized deposit pilot
items.description: SWIFT launched its new blockchain ledger that will host a tokenized bank deposit pilot for 17 major banks seeking faster cross-border payments.

items.title: Regulators invited Binance to seek new licenses after MiCA setback, co-CEO says
items.description: Binance is exploring new licensing paths into Europe while continuing to expand its regulatory footprint in Asia, says co-CEO Richard Teng.

items.title: Trump says Iran called to make a deal after U.S. strikes; adds it's unclear if war is back on
items.description: When asked whether the U.S. and Iran are returning to full-scale military conflict, Trump responded: "I don't know."

items.title: PepsiCo earnings miss estimates as North American consumers tighten their budgets
items.description: PepsiCo reported weaker-than-expected quarterly earnings despite strong international demand.

items.title: What AI companies want for the millions they're spending on elections
items.description: Lawmakers are working on AI legislation — and two major industry PACs are each pushing for their own version of regulation.

items.title: From 'dear Donald' to 'Trump trillion': Inside NATO chief Mark Rutte's U.S. strategy
items.description: NATO chief Mark Rutte lavished praise on U.S. President Donald Trump during a fractious summit in Turkey this week.

items.title: Ukraine’s drone playbook is wreaking havoc in Russia — and upending where NATO wants to invest
items.description: Ukraine’s deep drone strikes on Russian refineries are reshaping the war and pushing NATO toward a $40 billion counter-drone plan.

items.title: Why the world’s best-performing stock market this year fell into bear territory
items.description: The Kospi fell more than 5% on Wednesday, which brought it 20% below its June 19 record high, according to LSEG data.

items.title: Trump announces long-shot bid to get Supreme Court to rehear birthright citizenship case
items.description: Trump already asked the Supreme Court to reconsider denying him an appeal of a verdict holding him liable for sexually abusing and defaming E. Jean Carroll.

items.title: Platner quits Maine Senate race; Democrats set to pick new nominee
items.description: Maine Democrats must now replace Platner with less than four months until the midterm elections.

items.title: Bitcoin muted at $62k as US-Iran conflict re-escalates

items.title: Iran’s new order takes shape after Khamenei burial
items.description: Ali Khamenei’s funeral leaves son Mojtaba — about whom Iranians know little — to confront enormous challenges facing the regime

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=x2eJlMatzK4! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Schwab strategists warn of a major market shift: The era of easy index gains is officially over.
items.description: More geopolitical instability, more frequent supply shocks and macro volatility are facing investors in an era that’s just starting, says Charles Schwab.

items.title: I found a simple way to save money on frozen pizza. What’s your best money-saving trick?
items.description: “I changed my approach to cooking.”

items.title: My retirement savings will suffer if I delay Social Security. How do I choose between the two?
items.description: “By claiming earlier, I could preserve more of my portfolio and allow those assets to continue compounding.”

items.title: 10 stocks for momentum traders to consider buying — and 10 to avoid or sell short
items.description: These investments have a tendency to crash and burn

items.title: ‘The money would have been life-changing’: My father told my grandmother to slash my inheritance. Is that fair?
items.description: “I could have bought a home for what I hope will be a growing family.”

items.title: ‘I get $1,460 in Social Security’: My millionaire ex-husband, 74, refuses to pay alimony. What can I do?
items.description: “I have very little money, while my ex’s financial statements show assets in the millions.”

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Burnham must not succumb to a populist techlash
items.description: It would be a crime to lose the UK’s envied tech and life science status within Europe

items.title: Is Europe prepared for a world of extreme heat?
items.description: In an air-conditioned Luxembourg meeting, ministers call for a ‘business case’ for coping with the world’s fastest-warming continent

items.title: Devolution has no easy answers on ‘fairness’
items.description: The consensus backing more local power sharing rarely confronts fundamental trade-offs

items.title: FirstFT: Big Oil vs Trump
items.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Platner ends Senate campaign and why private assets could work for retail investors

items.title: China could be the US tech hedge
items.description: Diversification is not about finding a market without risk — it is about finding a market whose risks are different

items.title: European heatwave damps sales growth at clothing retailer Uniqlo
items.description: Hot weather forces some stores to close temporarily

items.title: Europe’s ‘China shock’ panic is ill-informed
items.description: EU industry is largely weathering the competition well

items.title: RayJay thinks SpaceX will soon be worth $10tn
items.description: ‘The defining industrial innovation of our generation,’ apparently

items.title: Help! I Refueled My Rental Car, but Enterprise Charged Me Anyway.
items.description: A traveler to Germany returned his vehicle with slightly more gas than it had when he picked it up. So why was he billed $131 to fill up the tank?

items.title: Can Xenom Turn Working Out Into a Sport?
items.description: Xenom, a new fitness company, wants to make weekend warriors feel like pros.

items.title: The World Cup’s Biggest Match Is Nike Vs. Adidas
items.description: Second place in the hearts and wallets of Americans isn’t good enough for the world’s largest sportswear companies, who bet big on the tournament.

items.title: Oil Prices Remain Elevated Amid Shipping Traffic Slowdown in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Fresh data showed that the number of ships moving through the Strait of Hormuz halved on Wednesday amid renewed fighting.

items.title: At Warsh’s First Fed Meeting, Some Officials Signaled Support for Raising Interest Rates
items.description: Minutes from Kevin M. Warsh’s first meeting as chairman showed heightened unease about inflation, which is running at a three-year high.

items.title: William D. Zabel, High-Flying Lawyer and Civil Rights Champion, Dies at 89
items.description: He brokered celebrity divorces and recovered billions for victims of Bernie Madoff’s Ponzi scheme. But he was proudest of helping to legalize interracial marriage.

items.title: Oil Market Calm Shattered by Fresh Hostilities Between US and Iran
items.description: Oil prices are likely to remain volatile as long as the United States and Iran are unable to resolve the status of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Air Canada Chooses Anko van der Werff, Who Speaks French, as CEO After Language Backlash
items.description: Michael Rousseau, who prompted widespread criticism with a largely English condolence video following a crash in March, will retire.
